nameMeanings.name: Pepys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: (i) relationship name from the Old French personal name Pepis. It is a nominative form of Pepon, a French version of Continental Germanic Pipo, and probably related to Old French Pepin. See Peppin . The personal name was extremely rare in medieval England. (ii) relationship name; alternatively it may mean; ‘Philip’s (son/relative/servant)’, from the genitive singular of Middle English * Pip, * Pep, side-forms perhaps of Phip, Phep, pet forms of Middle English Philip, Phelip. Compare Peppin , Phippen .

variants.name: Phippen
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): shortened form of the Anglo-Norman surname Fitzpain ‘son of Pain’ (see Payne ).

English: variant of Phipp with the West Midlands Middle English genitive singular -en instead of the more usual -s, see Phipps . Alternatively, perhaps from the unattested Middle English personal names Phippon or Phippin, which could be Old French pet forms of Philip , though they are not on record.
Irish: perhaps an altered form of Thigpen by association or confusion with the name in (1). The 17th-century Thigpen and Phippen families in NC were apparently related.

variants.name: Pipes
meanings: English: variant of Pipe , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William Pepys was born in 1464 as the son of Robert Pepys. He had at least 5 sons and 3 daughters with Margaret Whiston. He died in May 1519, in England, at the age of 55, and was buried in Cottenham, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: William Pepysは1464年にRobert Pepysの息子として生まれた。彼はMargaret Whistonとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1519年5月にイギリスで55歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアコッテンハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Pepys(은)는 1464년 Robert Pepys의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Margaret Whiston(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1519년 5월 영국에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 컷함에 묻혔다.
